data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification, OVERLAY): CLEAN / NOT-APPLICABLE (no findings).

T1 is an overlay that engages only when a bright line is crossed (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, novel architecture, performance/scalability target, migration). None fires for THIS plan — it is additive use of an established, already-shipped internal framework plus an already-adopted external dependency — so per T1's anti-FP clause ("a well-trodden pattern needs no prior-art search; not-applicable when no bright-line fires") the chunk PASSES.

Affirmations (T1):
- (a) Prior art considered / not reinventing: The plan does NOT build a new eval architecture — it builds a standing per-criterion suite ON the SHIPPED Inspect-AI eval seam (the plan explicitly says "NOW SHIPPED: Inspect-AI (src/rebar/llm/eval.py, .rebar/evals/<id>.eval.yaml specs, CI-gated) from epic e6d9"). Tool-verified: src/rebar/llm/eval.py exists with a designed grader-discipline regime (Inspect-AI behind the nava-rebar[eval] extra; deterministic-scorer-gates / llm-judge-reports-only; pinned cross-family grader; at_least(k) over epochs; frozen human-gold + Cohen's-kappa alignment — eval.py:1-25). Per-prompt specs already ship under src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ (plan-review-finder, plan-review-verifier, plan-review-isf-finder, code-quality). This is reuse of a well-trodden in-house pattern, not reinvention.
- (a) Anti-repackaging on ISF: the plan does NOT re-build ISF — it CONSUMES the criterion built in child 681b (which is closed / completion-verified PASS) and only ADDS ISF's eval cases (recall + false-fire) + wires ISF into the gate. The ownership split (681b owns criterion+prompt+session-log gating; 7284 owns suite/harness+labeled cases) is stated and backed by the relates_to link, so there is no duplicate-architecture risk for T1 to flag.
- (b) Novelty justified (Rule-of-Three / anti-repackaging): no NOVEL architectural pattern is introduced. The three case types (recall / false-accept / false-fire), the observed-FP taxonomy seeding, and the auto-label-breadth + human-adjudicated-gold-subset labeling approach are conventional eval-harness practice layered onto the existing seam — nothing to justify against an established approach.
- (c) Unverified capability assertions resolved: the load-bearing external-capability claim ("Inspect-AI supports per-prompt CI-gated evals") is not unverified — it is the shipped seam (eval.py + four shipped .eval.yaml specs; an ISF spec already ships at eval_specs/plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml with recall/false-fire cases, deterministic scorers, a pinned cross-family grader, and a gold_set). No "library supports X" assertion is left open.

No findings for T1.
